todaysEvents.summary: USS Spruance fires on, boards Iranian Touska in Gulf of Oman after 6-hour standoff — first US seizure of Iranian vessel; Iran vows 'swift retaliation'; Iranian FM: no decision on Islamabad talks (CNBC, Bloomberg, Al Jazeera, Apr 19).
todaysEvents.delta: Escalation: first US kinetic boarding of Iranian vessel — new threshold crossed; Iran formally suspends diplomacy in response

todaysEvents.summary: Ceasefire expires Apr 22; Trump: extension 'highly unlikely'; Iran not returning to Islamabad — no framework in place (CNN, NBC, Apr 20). Level 4 maintained; Level 5 threshold not yet crossed pending Iranian retaliation action.
todaysEvents.delta: Escalation: ceasefire expires at zero-deal; diplomacy suspended on both sides; 24-hour binary now active

todaysEvents.summary: Brent ~$99.50 est. (+3.7%; last confirmed $95.42 TradingEconomics Apr 20); WTI ~$94.70 est.; iTraxx Asia IG ~205bp est. (+15bp); ASEAN HY ~585bp est. (+20bp); tail risk 50% (Apr 21).
todaysEvents.delta: Markets price ceasefire expiry and Touska seizure: Brent approaches $100, credit spreads widen, tail risk peaks to 50%

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: Extraordinary last-minute concession before Apr 22 Wednesday evening deadline: Iran accepts partial deal framework via Pakistan back-channel despite Touska seizure standoff. Requires Iran to stand down retaliation threat + US to release Touska crew. Brent retraces toward $88-94/bbl; iTraxx Asia IG tightens below 185bp. Near-zero probability given Trump's 'highly unlikely' extension signal, Iran's suspension of talks, and active retaliation vow.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: Ceasefire lapses Apr 22 but both sides limit kinetic response. Iranian retaliation for Touska seizure is symbolic - targeting unoccupied infrastructure or US naval drills rather than crewed assets. IRGC reverts to toll-booth enforcement and harassment rather than full closure. US maintains blockade. Brent stabilises $95-115/bbl; JKM $21-26/MMBtu; iTraxx Asia IG 195-220bp. No deal but tacit off-ramp via backchannel keeps negotiation alive.

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: Ceasefire expires Apr 22; Iran executes retaliatory strike on US naval asset in response to Touska seizure; IRGC activates Gulf-wide closure — Hormuz fully blocked, Houthis reactivated in Red Sea. US-Israel air campaign resumes in force. Kharg Island seizure or Saudi Aramco strike brings GCC production under acute threat. Brent spikes $130-170/bbl; LNG markets seize; ASEAN recession risk acute. Gold surges past $5,500/oz.
